title: rhinorchis lehmanniana
intro: Rhinorchis lehmanniana, also known as the Golden Sedge or Golden Club Fern, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rhinorchis lehmanniana include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce fever, and soothe irritated throats. Rhinorchis lehmanniana can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing its leaves and roots in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting its active compounds in a solvent.
